title: Ensure that account 'Lockout threshold' is less than or equal  to '10'

input_row.title: Ensure that account 'Lockout threshold' is less than or equal  to '10'
input_row.description: The account lockout threshold determines how many failed login attempts are permitted prior to placing the account in a locked-out state and initiating a variable lockout duration.
input_row.rationale: Account lockout is a method of protecting against brute-force and password spray attacks. Once the lockout threshold has been exceeded, the account enters a locked- out state which prevents all login attempts for a variable duration. The lockout in combination with a reasonable duration reduces the total number of failed login attempts that a malicious actor can execute in a given period of time.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID. 3. Under Manage, select Security. 4. Under Manage, select Authentication methods. 5. Under Manage, select Password protection. 6. Ensure that Lockout threshold is set to 10 or fewer. Audit from PowerShell Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Policy.ReadWrite.AuthenticationMethod" $p = (Get-MgPolicyAuthenticationMethodsPolicy).PasswordProtection if ($p.LockoutThreshold -gt 10) { Update-MgPolicyAuthenticationMethodsPolicy -BodyParameter @{ passwordProtection = @{ lockoutThreshold = 10 lockoutDuration = [Math]::Max($p.LockoutDuration, 60) } } } Disconnect-MgGraph Audit from Azure CLI az rest --method get \ --url 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/policies/authenticationMethodsPolicy' \ --query 'passwordProtection.lockoutThreshold'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID. 3. Under Manage, select Security. 4. Under Manage, select Authentication methods. 5. Under Manage, select Password protection. 6. Set the Lockout threshold to 10 or fewer. 7. Click Save. Remediate from PowerShell Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Policy.ReadWrite.AuthenticationMethod" Update-MgPolicyAuthenticationMethodsPolicy -PasswordProtection @{ LockoutThreshold = 10 LockoutDuration = "PT1M" } Remediate from Azure CLI az rest --method patch \ --url 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/policies/authenticationMethodsPolicy' \ --headers 'Content-Type=application/json' \ --body '{"passwordProtection":{"lockoutThreshold":10,"lockoutDuration":"PT1M"}}'
input_row.impact: If account lockout threshold is set too low (less than 3), users may experience frequent lockout events and the resulting security alerts may contribute to alert fatigue. If account lockout threshold is set too high (more than 10), malicious actors can programmatically execute more password attempts in a given period of time.
input_row.default_value: By default, Lockout threshold is set to 10.
input_row.additional_information: NOTE: The variable number for failed login attempts allowed before lockout is prescribed by many security and compliance frameworks. The appropriate setting for this variable should be determined by the most restrictive security or compliance framework that your organization follows.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Navigate to IAM Management Console:**
   - Open the AWS Management Console and select "IAM" from the Services menu.
   
2. **Verify Account Password Policy:**
   - In the IAM dashboard, click on "Account settings" located in the left navigation pane.
   - Under the "Password policy" section, look for "Maximum password age" and "Minimum password length" settings.

3. **Check for Lockout Threshold:**
   - Note that AWS IAM does not natively enforce a lockout threshold like Azure. However, you should verify if your organization has implemented any custom solutions (using AWS Lambda, CloudWatch, etc.) for account lockout management.

4. **Consult Implementation Documentation:**
   - Review any internal documentation or configurations that your organization may have set up to manage login attempts.

5. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - If a custom solution exists, ensure that the lockout threshold does not exceed 10 failed login attempts (if applicable).

6. **How to Confirm Compliance:**
   - Confirm compliance by ensuring any existing custom implementations are documented and that they adhere to the desired lockout threshold specified by your organization's security policy. If no custom implementation exists, document that there is no applicable control for this specific audit and ensure to communicate this with the security team.
